context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T11:08Z after Homestead/Studio extensibility and surface diagnostics slices. Branch develop is clean and ahead of origin/develop by 36 local commits. Disk remains constrained but stable at about 7.5GB free; repo is about 1.5GB and node_modules about 975MB, so no cleanup was run because current artifacts are small and cleanup would increase rebuild cost.
current_tasks: Completed the multi-surface extensibility path from manifest declaration to Homestead mounting, DOM identity, telemetry, SDK inspection, and Studio diagnostics. Current HEAD before this handoff update: 52226e6e docs(studio): record mounted surface diagnostics.

The local branch is ahead of origin/develop by 36 commits. Recent important commits include: 9209385f feat(app-dev): render mounted surface inspector; a162e0e7 feat(homestead): expose mounted surface inspector; 1705b31b feat(homestead): emit surface mount telemetry; f4e628d3 feat(homestead): preserve extension surface mount identity; 44c2cfca feat(ds): add reusable UI surface primitives; 8dc78bce refactor(homestead): render streams with ds primitives; a37c694d refactor(app-dev): consume ds stream workbench primitives; b6e7b92b feat(homestead): link streams workbench in shell nav; 1117f3e1 feat(app-dev): add stream workbench page; fbabc568 feat(app-dev): seed opt-in stream demo.
User clarified short-term UI experiments should be in Homestead plus Studio app `apps/dev`, not `me` or `social`. Docs were updated to anchor this direction.

`@refarm.dev/ds` is now the home for reusable framework-agnostic visual primitives. Added DS classes for surfaces, panels, pills, badges, cards, buttons, code chips, workbench layout, stacks, and clusters. Homestead and apps/dev now consume these classes for stream/status/workbench UI while keeping domain behavior local.

Homestead stream UI now consumes DS primitives and renders both statusbar pill and `streams` panel from generic `StreamSession` and `StreamChunk` observations. BrowserSyncClient remains schema-neutral.

`apps/dev` now has an opt-in stream demo: enable with the statusbar toggle, `/?stream-demo`, `/?streamDemo=1`, or localStorage key `refarm:studio:stream-demo=1`. It seeds generic stream nodes through Tractor to exercise the same Homestead render path real agent streams use.
`apps/dev` has a `/streams` workbench page linked from Homestead nav. Build confirmed `/streams/index.html`.

Homestead surface mounting now preserves mount identity: wrappers get `data-refarm-plugin-id`, `data-refarm-slot-id`, `data-refarm-mount-source`, and for extension surfaces also `data-refarm-surface-layer`, `data-refarm-surface-kind`, `data-refarm-surface-id`.
Homestead emits `ui:surface_mounted` telemetry for each mount with pluginId, slotId, mountSource, and surface metadata. This creates an auditable manifest -> slot -> DOM path.

Homestead SDK exports `listMountedHomesteadSurfaces(...)` via subpath `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-inspector`. `apps/dev` consumes that helper to render a small mounted-surface inspector after shell setup.

Validation passed in this stream: `npm --prefix packages/ds run test`, `npm --prefix packages/ds run build`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead test`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run type-check`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run build`, `npm --prefix apps/dev test`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run type-check`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run build`, `npm run agent:streaming:studio`, `npm run project:validate --silent`, and `git diff --check`.
blockers: Disk is still 99% used on the host mount with about 7.5GB free. The repo is about 1.5GB; current generated artifacts are not large enough to justify cleanup. Recheck disk before broad builds and use cleanup only if free space falls near 2-3GB or new large artifacts appear.
Rust target dirs were previously removed by clean:heavy. Broad Rust checks will rebuild from scratch and may consume disk. Use scoped Rust commands from AGENTS.md only if Rust is touched.

Full tractor-ts unit suite remains known-noisy when WASM fixtures are missing after cleanup. Prefer focused tests or `npm run agent:streaming:clients` / `npm run agent:streaming:studio` unless intentionally rebuilding fixtures.

`apps/dev` importing from the Homestead SDK barrel can pull `Shell` and locale imports through dist. For inspector-only consumers use the explicit subpath `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-inspector`, which was added for this reason.
pi-lens/auto-fix may warn on Markdown or innerHTML usage. Re-read files after auto-format/fix warnings and avoid new unsafe innerHTML in tests/helpers.
next_actions: First command after compaction: `git status --branch --short && df -h /workspaces/refarm && git log --oneline -16`.

Before pushing, run an economical aggregate gate for touched areas: `npm run agent:streaming:studio && npm --prefix packages/ds run test && npm --prefix packages/ds run build && npm run project:validate --silent && git diff --check`.
Consider pushing the 36 local commits to origin/develop as a checkpoint, then watch CI. This is a large local-ahead stack now.

Next technical slice: make the Studio mounted-surface inspector update after `ui:surface_mounted` telemetry events instead of only after initial shell setup. Keep it in `apps/dev` and avoid `me`/`social`.
Next extensibility slice: add an example plugin/surface that mounts into `streams` or `main` so the inspector displays a non-zero real extension surface without hand-crafted DOM.

Next governance slice: formalize deeper surface activation trust checks beyond slot-level capability filtering. Current gate filters declared surface capabilities before mounting, but runtime trust still belongs deeper in plugin host activation.
Next DS slice: continue moving generic inline shell/help styles into DS primitives, but keep stream-specific logic and text in Homestead/apps-dev.
Run `npm run clean:light --silent` only at checkpoint boundaries if `.turbo` or incremental artifacts grow. Use heavy cleanup only near critical disk pressure.
open_questions: Should the Studio surface inspector become a `/surfaces` page, remain statusbar diagnostics, or both?
Should `ui.slots` become deprecated once `extensions.surfaces` has a real executable plugin example and inspector coverage?
What should be the first real plugin-provided `homestead` surface: stream panel/editor, plugin management panel, or project memory surface?
Where exactly should deeper surface trust be enforced: before DOM wrapper creation in Homestead, inside PluginHost activation, or both with different responsibilities?
key_decisions_pending: Push timing for the now-large local commit stack ahead of origin/develop.
First real executable multi-surface plugin example location: `examples/` vs workspace package.
Runtime trust boundary for manifest-declared surfaces.
Whether daily-driver parity checklist should be converted into canonical .project tasks after the current surface path stabilizes.
